A banded surface features alternating parallel stripes of varying colors or materials, creating a layered, striped look.

A blotchy surface has irregular patches or spots of contrasting colors or shades, creating a splotchy, uneven appearance.

A braided surface consists of interwoven strands, crossing over each other in a crisscross or woven pattern.

A bubbly surface is covered in small, rounded bubbles or raised bumps, resembling foam or air pockets.

A bumpy surface has irregular raised areas, creating a rough, uneven texture with small protrusions.

A chequered surface features a grid of alternating squares, usually in two contrasting colors, like a chessboard.

A cobwebbed surface is crisscrossed with thin, delicate, and intertwined lines resembling a web.

A cracked surface shows visible fissures and breaks, with varying sizes of gaps, suggesting dryness or wear.

A crosshatched surface has intersecting lines that form a grid or mesh-like pattern with layered overlaps.

A crystalline surface has sharp, angular facets, often reflecting light, giving it a jagged and geometric appearance.

A dotted surface features small, round dots spaced evenly or randomly across, giving a speckled appearance.

A fibrous surface consists of thin, thread-like strands, often resembling a rough or woven fabric.

A flecked surface has small, scattered spots of contrasting color, creating a speckled or peppered effect.

A freckled surface has tiny, irregular spots spread unevenly, similar to freckles on skin.

A frilly surface is adorned with delicate, wavy or ruffled edges, often giving a soft, decorative look.

A gauzy surface is thin, semi-transparent, and lightweight, resembling fine, airy fabric.

A grid texture features a pattern of intersecting horizontal and vertical lines, forming squares or rectangles.

A grooved surface has parallel indented lines or channels carved into it, creating a ridged feel.

A honeycombed surface has a pattern of small, hexagonal cavities, resembling a beehive.

An interlaced surface is made of overlapping strands woven together in a crisscross pattern.

A knitted surface consists of interlocking loops of yarn or thread, creating a flexible, woven appearance.

A lacelike surface is delicate, with intricate, open patterns resembling lace fabric.

A lined surface features thin, straight, parallel lines running across it, creating a simple striped pattern.

A marbled surface shows swirled patterns of different colors or shades, resembling natural stone veins.

A matted surface is densely packed, tangled, or flattened, giving it a dull, compressed appearance.

A meshed surface consists of open, crisscrossing strands that form a grid or net-like pattern.

A paisley surface has curved, teardrop-shaped patterns filled with intricate, decorative details.

A perforated surface has small, regularly spaced holes punched through, creating a breathable effect.

A pitted surface is dotted with small, shallow depressions or indentations, giving it a worn look.

A pleated surface features evenly folded sections, creating raised ridges and valleys.

A polka-dotted surface has evenly spaced, round dots of uniform size spread across it.

A porous surface has tiny holes or spaces, often seen in materials like sponge or stone.

A potholed surface has large, irregular cavities or pits, often resulting from erosion or damage.

A scaly surface consists of overlapping, rough plates or scales, resembling reptilian skin.

A smeared surface appears as though something has been spread unevenly, leaving streaks or smudges.

A spiralled surface features a continuous, coiling pattern that winds in a circular, vortex-like manner.

A sprinkled surface is covered with small, scattered particles or specks, like sprinkles on a dessert.

A stained surface has patches of discoloration, usually irregular and caused by liquids or wear.

A stratified surface shows distinct layers or bands of different materials or colors, often horizontal.

A striped surface has long, parallel bands of alternating colors or textures, creating a repetitive pattern.

A studded surface is covered with small, raised protrusions or decorative metal points.

A swirly surface features flowing, looping patterns, resembling waves or spirals in motion.

A veined surface has thin, branching lines running through it, resembling the veins in leaves or marble.

A waffled surface has a crisscross pattern of ridges and grooves, similar to a waffle's surface.

A woven surface is made of interlacing threads or strips, forming a crisscrossing pattern of fabric or material.

A wrinkled surface is covered in small, irregular folds or creases, giving it a crumpled appearance.

A zigzagged surface features sharp, angular lines alternating in a back-and-forth pattern.
